problem: Why does the immune system attack the body's own tissue in autoimmune disease?
mechanism: Autophagy activation to clear misfolded proteins
hypothesis: Why does the immune system attack the body's own tissue in autoimmune disease? remains unsolved because previous research missed the role of Autophagy. Autophagy activation to clear misfolded proteins directly addresses this.

experiment.protocol: Mouse model knockout study with 14 month follow-up. Control group receives placebo/sham.

wisdom: What we observe is not nature itself but nature exposed to our method of questioning. — Heisenberg
